claimText: Under Co-Lending Arrangements (CLAs), Regulated Entities (REs) such as banks and Non-bank Financial Companies (NBFCs) can jointly extend credit to borrowers.
evidenceExcerpt: The RBI has issued revised directions for Co-Lending Arrangements (CLAs) between Regulated Entities (REs) like banks and Non-bank Financial Companies (NBFCs). Under these arrangements, REs can jointly extend credit to borrowers.
examUse: This can be used in a statement-based question to test the understanding of the co-lending model and the entities involved.
distractorHooks: Only banks can participate in CLAs.
CLAs are for deposit mobilization, not credit extension.

claimText: In a Co-Lending Arrangement, each participating lending entity is mandated to retain a minimum of 10% of the loan's share on its own books.
evidenceExcerpt: Key revised guidelines state that each lending entity must retain a minimum of 10% share of the loans on its books.
examUse: This specific percentage can be used in a statement-based question to check factual recall about the co-lending norms.
distractorHooks: The entire loan must be on the books of the bank.

claimText: Each lender participating in a Co-Lending Arrangement can claim Priority Sector Lending (PSL) status for its respective share of the loan, provided the loan qualifies under PSL norms.
evidenceExcerpt: Additionally, each lender can claim Priority Sector Lending (PSL) status for its respective share of the loan, provided the loan qualifies under PSL norms.
examUse: This can be used in a question testing the application and benefits of PSL norms within the co-lending framework.
distractorHooks: Only the bank's share of the loan qualifies for PSL status.
The entire co-lent loan automatically gets PSL status regardless of the end-use.
PSL status is not applicable to any portion of a co-lent loan.
